ground_truth.title: Best practices & strategies for gaming companies to boost their business performance
ground_truth.author: Gokul Sarkar
ground_truth.main_content:
The emergence of mobile gaming has made cloud computing essential for delivering seamless, cross-platform experiences. Combined with data analytics and AI, these technologies personalize gameplay, analyze player preferences, and provide real-time insights, keeping users immersed and engaged. Together, they ensure minimal downtime and set new standards in the gaming industry.

According to EY, 82% of gamers would skip purchasing new gaming hardware in favor of the cloud if connectivity guarantees performance. Cloud gaming is expected to reach a value of $96.67 billion by 2034 – up from just $2.27 billion in 2024.

Five key challenges facing the online gaming industry: data overload with over 3 billion online gamers worldwide and companies generating around 50 terabytes of gaming data daily; user engagement difficulties especially with legacy games; monetization issues with 83% of mobile games failing within the first 3 years; lack of gaming features due to costly development processes; and lack of real-time streaming functionalities including limited bandwidth and load balancing issues.

Six best practices for business growth: deploy scalable cloud infrastructure with auto-scaling capabilities using AWS Elastic Beanstalk and Google Kubernetes Engine; optimize network infrastructure using AWS Global Accelerator and Google Cloud CDN to reduce data latency; implement a multi-cloud strategy across Google Cloud, AWS, and MS Azure; leverage AI and machine learning for personalizing gaming experience and enhancing monetization; enhance gaming experience with AR/VR technologies; execute data modernization by migrating from legacy systems to cloud-powered platforms.
with: The emergence of mobile gaming has made cloud computing essential for delivering seamless, cross-platform experiences.
According to EY, 82% of gamers would skip purchasing new gaming hardware in favor of the cloud if connectivity guarantees performance.
Cloud gaming is expected to reach a value of $96.67 billion by 2034 – up from just $2.27 billion in 2024.
With the use of AI-powered gaming analytics, companies can now turn around the massive gaming data for their advantage through real-time actionable insights.
Here are 6 best practices and strategies for online gaming companies to accelerate their business growth.
